title: Setting Up Management Fees on Properties

content.intro: Management fees can be configured per-property to track what your agency charges landlords for managing their property. These fees flow through to RentGoose for accurate landlord statements.

sections.title: How to Set Up a Management Fee

steps.title: Open the Property
steps.content: Navigate to the Properties tab and open the property you want to configure a management fee for.

steps.title: Click Edit or Open Property Settings
steps.content: Click the Edit button or access the property settings panel to enter edit mode.

steps.title: Find the Management Fee Section
steps.content: Scroll to the Management Fee section within the property settings.

steps.title: Enter the Fee
steps.content: Enter the management fee as a percentage (e.g. 10% of monthly rent) or as a fixed monthly amount (e.g. £80 per month). Choose whichever method your agency charges.

steps.title: Select VAT Treatment
steps.content: Select whether the fee is inclusive or exclusive of VAT. If your agency is VAT registered, entering the fee exclusive of VAT allows PropertyGoose to add VAT automatically on landlord statements.

steps.title: Save
steps.content: Click Save to apply the management fee to the property.

sections.title: Management Fees in RentGoose
sections.content: Management fees set at the property level are used by RentGoose to calculate deductions from rent receipts and produce accurate landlord statements showing gross rent, fee deductions, and net payment.

sections.title: Set Fees Per Property for Variable Rates
sections.content: If you charge different management fees for different landlords or property types, set the fee at the property level rather than relying on a global default. This gives you full flexibility per portfolio.
